description: Dedicated search page at /search with filters, recent searches, popular searches, and full-page search experience
steps: Create src/app/search/page.tsx as a full-page search experience. Add metadata with title 'Search Anime - Find Your Next Watch | AniYume', canonical '/search'. The page should have a SearchPageClient component.

Create src/components/SearchPageClient.tsx as a 'use client' component with: (1) Large search input at top with debounced real-time results (reuse /api/search endpoint), (2) Filter section below with dropdowns for: genre (all genres from schema), year range (2000-2026), minimum score (slider or select: 6.0, 7.0, 8.0, 9.0), format (TV/Movie/OVA/ONA/Special), airing status (All/Airing/Finished/Upcoming), (3) Results grid using AnimeCard-like display.
In SearchPageClient.tsx, implement recent searches stored in localStorage (key: 'aniyume-recent-searches', max 10 items). Show recent searches as clickable chips when input is focused and empty.

In SearchPageClient.tsx, show popular/suggested searches when input is empty: ['Naruto', 'Bleach', 'One Piece', 'Attack on Titan', 'Dragon Ball Z', 'Death Note', 'Fullmetal Alchemist', 'My Hero Academia', 'Demon Slayer', 'Jujutsu Kaisen']. Display as clickable pills.

Create a new API route src/app/api/search/advanced/route.ts that accepts query params: q (search text), genre, yearMin, yearMax, scoreMin, format, status, page, limit. Build a Prisma where clause combining all filters. Return paginated results with total count. Use the cached() wrapper from lib/cache.ts.

In SearchPageClient.tsx, implement empty state showing trending anime (fetch from /api/anime?sort=popularity&limit=10), and 'no results' state with suggestions like 'Try a different spelling' or 'Browse by genre' with genre links.
Make genre tags in search results clickable - clicking a genre tag should add it as a filter.
Update src/app/sitemap.ts to include { url: SITE_URL + '/search', changeFrequency: 'daily', priority: 0.8 }.
Update src/components/Sidebar.tsx: change the Search sidebar item href from '/top/most-popular' to '/search'.
acceptance_criteria: /search page renders with search input and filter controls
Typing in search triggers debounced API call and shows results
Filters (genre, year, score, format, status) work via the advanced search API
Recent searches persist in localStorage and display as chips
Popular searches shown when input is empty
No results state shows helpful suggestions
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/anime-guide-engine && npx prisma generate && npx next build
findings: Build passes cleanly. The /search page is static, /api/search/advanced is dynamic. Sidebar search link now points to /search. Sitemap includes /search entry. No test suite exists in this project.
